title: Hyabb

'''Hyabb''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Krenher sector/Legends|Krenher sector]]. It lay on the [[Hyabb-Twith Corridor]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], which connected it to the planet [[Twith]]. Hyabb and Twith were engaged in a continuous [[Hyabb–Twith war|war]] lasting nearly since the [[Galactic Constitution Conference|foundation]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and were both members of the Republic by [[17,000 BBY]]. During the [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]], Hyabb remained loyal to the world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in its struggle against the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], with which Twith had allied itself.

[[Standard decade/Legends|Decades]] before [[4050 BBY]], both Hyabb and Twith came under the oppression of the [[Nelori Marauders]], a group of bandits that established their [[Nelori stronghold|base]] in the Hyabb-Twith Corridor. Due to the hyperlane serving as a no-man's-land zone for the two rival worlds, the Marauders preyed unopposed on both Hyabb and Twith and surrounding [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. The Republic's [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] eventually [[Hyabb-Twith Campaigns|defeated]] the Nelori, freeing both Hyabb and Twith from the outlaws' influence.

==Description==
Hyabb was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Hyabb">{{CSWECite|II|69|Hyabb}}</ref> located in the [[Hyabb system/Legends|Hyabb system]], a part of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Krenher sector/Legends|Krenher sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated at the endpoint of the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Hyabb-Twith Corridor]],<ref name="Corridor" /> which connected it to the [[Voon system/Legends|Voon system]] and, ultimately, the [[Twith system/Legends|Twith system]].<ref name="TEA Hyabb" />

==History==
===Old wars===
Hyabb and its neighboring planet [[Twith]] were at a state of continuous [[Hyabb–Twith war|war]] that had lasted almost since the [[Galactic Constitution Conference|foundation]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="CSWE Hyabb" /> in [[25,053 BBY]].<ref name="SWTORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> As a result, the Hyabb-Twith Corridor essentially became a virtually empty no-man's-land region between the two heavily-armed worlds. Neither Hyabb or Twith dared to risk repercussions from the other by dispatching [[Patrol ship/Legends|patrol ships]] into the disputed territory, and the neighboring [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] were actively dissuaded from entering the zone, as well.<ref name="TotJC">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref>

[[File:HyabbTwith-EGTW.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Hyabb and Twith were on opposing sides of the early Alsakan Conflicts.]]
Both Hyabb and Twith fell within the borders of the Republic by [[17,000 BBY]], and they found themselves on the opposing sides of the early [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]]. A series of wars that [[First Alsakan Conflict|erupted]] around [[17,018 BBY]], the Conflicts saw Twith ally with the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] in its struggle against the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for the status of the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. Hyabb, however, remained loyal to Coruscant.<ref name="EGTW" />

===The Nelori Marauders===
[[Standard decade/Legends|Decades]] before [[4050 BBY]],<ref name="4000 date">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' establishes that the [[Nelori Marauders]] were defeated in the [[Hyabb-Twith Campaigns]] "[[Standard decade/Legends|decades]]" prior to [[Arca Jeth]] becoming a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. In addition, ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' both establish that Jeth had attained that rank by the [[Time/Legends|time]] he participated in the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Battle of Lorell|victory]] over the [[Lorell Raiders]]. Since ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' dates the defeat of the Lorell Raiders to approximately [[4050 BBY]], the Hyabb-Twith Campaigns must have occurred decades prior to that date.</ref> the [[Nelori Marauders]],<ref name="TotJC" /> a band of warriors,<ref name="CSWE Nelori">{{CSWECite|II|369|Nelori Marauders}}</ref> [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]],<ref name="CSWE Dachat">{{CSWECite|I|160|Dachat}}</ref> and outlaws, were driven off its [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Dachat]]. The Nelori Marauders took advantage of the tensions between Hyabb and Twith and established their new [[Nelori stronghold|main base]] on the planet [[Voon/Legends|Voon]]. Since neither Hyabb or Twith could afford to risk taking any action against the bandits,<ref name="TotJC" /> the Nelori preyed upon the governments of both planets,<ref name="CSWE Dachat" /> oppressing both of the worlds.<ref name="CSWE Hyabb" /><ref name="CSWE Twith">{{CSWECite|III|270|Twith}}</ref>

Furthermore, the Nelori Marauders raided [[Starship/Legends|starships]] both on the Hyabb-Twith Corridor<ref name="CSWE Dachat" /> and in the nearby systems. When the Nelori sphere of control began to extend deeper into the Republic's territory, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] dispatched a task force of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] to eliminate the threat posed by the Marauders. Following a series of confrontations known as the [[Hyabb-Twith Campaigns]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were victorious over the Nelori, shattering the latter's sphere of influence<ref name="TotJC" /> and liberating both Hyabb and Twith.<ref name="CSWE Hyabb" /><ref name="CSWE Twith" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hyabb was introduced in the ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', a [[1996]] sourcebook authored by [[George R. Strayton]] and published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="TotJC" /> It was given an entry in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which specified that Hyabb was a planet.<ref name="CSWE Hyabb" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Hyabb system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square K-9.<ref name="TEA Hyabb" />
